title: Almost Ready
subtitle: Final weeks of preparation
description: By week 36, your baby is considered late preterm and continues final preparations for birth. Organs are mature, and your baby may settle deeper into the pelvis.
what_to_expect: Fetal movements remain strong but feel more restricted
Baby may move lower into the pelvis
You may experience increased pelvic pressure and frequent urination
care_tips: Maintain a balanced and nutrient-rich diet
Stay hydrated and follow a consistent routine
Rest adequately and prepare for delivery

medical_checkups.note: Weekly monitoring may begin at this stage.

commonly_suggested.name: Routine Prenatal Checkup
commonly_suggested.purpose: Tracks maternal health and fetal position

medical_checkups.guidance: Your doctor may begin weekly visits to closely monitor you and your baby.

important_note: Your baby is in the final stage of preparation for birth.
reassurance: You are very close to meeting your baby.
medical_disclaimer: Consult your healthcare provider if you notice reduced movements or any unusual symptoms.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining stable blood sugar levels is essential as delivery approaches.
diabetes.medical_focus: Blood sugar levels may be closely monitored during this stage.
diabetes.reassurance: Consistent management supports a safe delivery.

anemia.personalized_description: Adequate iron supports oxygen supply during this final preparation phase.
care_tips: Pair with vitamin C for better absorption
anemia.medical_focus: Hemoglobin levels may be monitored during routine tests.
anemia.reassurance: Supporting your nutrition helps maintain your strength.

hypertension.personalized_description: Stable blood pressure is critical as you approach delivery.
hypertension.medical_focus: Blood pressure monitoring may be more frequent.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and steady routine supports your well-being.

thyroid.personalized_description: Balanced thyroid levels support stability during the final stage.
thyroid.medical_focus: Thyroid function may be monitored during ongoing evaluations.
thyroid.reassurance: Consistency helps maintain a healthy balance.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: As you enter the final weeks, managing multiple conditions requires consistent and mindful care.
priority_tips: Maintain a balanced and regular lifestyle
Avoid sudden dietary or lifestyle changes
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Steady care supports a smooth and safe delivery.
